title: Functions 5
description: Rust distinguishes between statements (which perform actions) and expressions (which return values). Understanding this distinction is key.
instructions: Fix the function body so it returns the correct value. Remember that statements don't return values.
hints: This code actually compiles! But look at the pattern carefully.
The last line `result` without a semicolon is an expression and will be returned.
This is correct Rust. An alternative is to just write `num * num` as the last expression.
explanation: In Rust, statements perform actions but don't return values (like `let x = 5;`), while expressions evaluate to a value (like `5 + 3`). A block `{}` can contain statements and ends with an optional expression (without semicolon) which becomes the block's value. This is why `num * num` (no semicolon) at the end of a function returns that value.
